audit_items.proof_trace: testtavl.c is a standalone test binary (Makefile.in builds it as its own executable from testtavl.o; it has its own main() and reads from stdin) — LDAP network clients cannot invoke it, making the 'external-reachable via LDAP' reachability classification incorrect. The only code-quality gap is the unchecked strdup() return at line 88, which could insert NULL avl_data into the tree and later crash printf/strcmp; however, this requires malloc failure, which is not attacker-controllable. All other pointer dereferences (fgets results at lines 74/85/94, AVL traversal n != NULL guard, ldap_tavl_find result check, ravl_print root guards) are properly null-checked.
